content.problemStatement:
Chef has discovered a mysterious n×nn \times nn×n binary grid called `binaryGrid`. Each cell in this grid represents either water (0) or land (1). 

Chef wants to create the largest possible island by changing at most one water cell (0) into land (1). An island is defined as a group of land cells connected 4-directionally (up, down, left, right).

Help Chef find the size of the largest island he can form after performing at most one such change. If no changes are needed or possible, Chef still wants to know the size of the largest existing island.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindLargestIslandfindLargestIslandfindLargestIsland - This function calculates the size of the largest island (connected group of 1s) in a binary grid after changing at most one zero to one.

Parameters
binaryGridbinaryGridbinaryGrid : A 2D matrix representing the grid, where each cell contains either 0 (water) or 1 (land).
The grid dimensions are gridRowSize×gridColumnSizegridRowSize \times gridColumnSizegridRowSize×gridColumnSize, with gridRowSizegridRowSizegridRowSize being the number of rows and gridColumnSizegridColumnSizegridColumnSize the number of columns.
Return Value
Returns a number representing the size (number of cells) of the largest island possible after changing at most one 0 to 1.
If no land exists, returns 0.
Constraints
1≤n≤5001 \leq n \leq 5001≤n≤500
binaryGrid[i][j]∈{0,1}binaryGrid[i][j] \in \{0, 1\}binaryGrid[i][j]∈{0,1} for 0≤i,j<n0 \leq i, j < n0≤i,j<n
Help Chef find the largest island size he can achieve with his clever transformation!


Input Format

The first line contains TTT test cases and in each test case:

The first line contains an integer nnn — the size of the grid (number of rows and columns).
The next nnn lines each contain nnn integers separated by spaces within []s, representing the `binaryGrid`. Each integer is either 0 or 1.




Output Format

Print a single integer — the size of the largest island Chef can obtain by changing at most one 0 to 1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
t=1: All zeros, flipping one cell creates an island of size 1.
t=2: Flipping the zero at (1,1) connects two islands of size 2 and 2, forming an island of size 4.
t=3: Flipping the zero at (0,1) or (1,2) or (2,1) connects 3 islands of size 1 each, resulting in an island of size 3.

solution.timeComplexity: O(gridRowSize * gridColumnSize), since each cell is processed a few times.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(gridRowSize * gridColumnSize) to store island IDs and sizes.
